Chronology of every recorded fact about [[Incarnations]], in reading order. Generated from the same verified fact store as the main article.

== House of Blades ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 14''' — The Trees maintain the seal on the Incarnations.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 14''' — Incarnations are entities that can be sealed.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 23''' — The Incarnations shake their cage and make Territories tremble.}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 6''' — Incarnations are entities that were previously imprisoned and are feared as monsters that spread madness and chaos.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 6''' — Incarnations are noble creatures with incalculable power that go mad if they stay in the world too long.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 7''' — An Incarnation from Valinhall has escaped and is on a rampage.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 8''' — Incarnations become trapped if they stay too long in the Territories that created them.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 15''' — Incarnations are described as a Traveler's power gone rampant.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 16''' — The emergence of the Incarnations could unseat the power of Talos's father.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 17''' — Incarnations are beings that the people of Enosh released.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 22''' — The Incarnations are a group that Alin believes should not be released.}}

== City of Light ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 2''' — Incarnations act according to their nature when unbound.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 6''' — Simon can stop Incarnations from hurting ordinary people.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 10''' — The Incarnations are described as walking natural disasters.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 17''' — Incarnations can meet attacks from Travelers without paying attention.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 18''' — At least eight of the original nine Incarnations were lured to transform outside of a Territory.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 19''' — The Incarnations use physical abuse, phantom noises, and illusions to torment Indirial.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 20''' — Incarnations are notoriously lacking in self-control.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 25''' — Some Incarnations are little better than monsters.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 27''' — Caela states that the Incarnations are not on the battlefield.}}

[[Category:Timelines]]
